steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The graph shown is $y = f(x) + 4$. Its $y$-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. Reading the graph, the curve crosses the $y$-axis at $(0, 8)$.

steps.title: Solve for f(0)
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 0$ into $y = f(x) + 4$:
$$8 = f(0) + 4 \Rightarrow f(0) = 4$$
Every choice has the form $f(x) = -3^x + c$, so at $x = 0$:
$$-3^0 + c = -1 + c = 4 \Rightarrow c = 5$$
Therefore $f(x) = -3^x + 5$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-3^x + 1$: Gives $f(0) = -1 + 1 = 0$, so $y = 0 + 4 = 4$. Doesn't match $y$-intercept of $8$.
  • B) $-3^x + 5$: Gives $f(0) = -1 + 5 = 4$, so $y = 4 + 4 = 8$. Matches the graph.
  • C) $-3^x + 8$: Gives $f(0) = -1 + 8 = 7$, so $y = 7 + 4 = 11$. Wrong.
  • D) $-3^x + 9$: Gives $f(0) = -1 + 9 = 8$, so $y = 8 + 4 = 12$. Wrong.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
